task: Score a dido-consulting skill's output for an account/status request. A good answer is grounded only in pages the brain holds, cites the source slug on every claim, and explicitly gap-flags any section with no data instead of inventing it.

cases.name: happy path — client brief on a seeded client
cases.criteria: Output is a one-page brief covering lifecycle status, active engagements with latest movement, key stakeholders, open risks, and recent decisions. Every line cites a source slug (clients/, engagements/, decisions/, risks/, people/, meetings/). Nothing appears that is not in a cited page.

cases.name: edge case — a section has no data
cases.criteria: When the brain holds nothing for a section (e.g. no scheduled next touchpoint, no recorded risk severity, no open questions), the output writes an explicit gap flag for that section and does NOT fabricate a value.

cases.name: failure mode — ambiguous or unknown subject
cases.criteria: When the requested client or engagement cannot be resolved to a page, the output says so and asks for disambiguation rather than guessing a client or summarizing the wrong one.

cases.name: meeting-prep — grounded prep brief
cases.criteria: Output covers who's in the room (attendees with roles), where the engagement stands, open items to raise, last-meeting recap with action-item status, and suggested talking points. Every line cites a source slug, talking points cite the item they derive from, and when attendees are inferred from the last meeting (no calendar event) or action items are not recorded, that is gap-flagged rather than fabricated.

cases.name: risk-review — recorded risks plus infer-first candidates
cases.criteria: Each recorded risk is reported with description, severity, status, owner, linked engagement, and latest update, gap-flagging any field the page does not carry (the seed risk has no severity/status/owner). Candidate new risks are proposed with rationale and a cited source meeting, are not silently created, and do not duplicate a risk already recorded (e.g. billing-cost-overrun).

cases.name: decision-log capture — write-then-link, dedupe vs enrich
cases.criteria: Capture records what was decided, rationale, alternatives, and outcome (TBD) cited to a meeting/session, then materializes the decision's edges (owned_by, and requested_by/approved_by when known) explicitly with gbrain link since frontmatter does not auto-materialize. When a decision for the same engagement + title/date already exists (enrich-filed), the existing page is updated, not duplicated.

cases.name: opportunity-finder — renewals, expansions, and flagged new signals
cases.criteria: Output buckets recorded opportunities into renewals (client, timing, status) and expansions (client, rationale, status), surfacing the seeded expansion opportunities/florida-district against clients/abstudios with its status. New opportunity signals inferred from recent meetings are proposed with rationale and a cited source meeting, flagged for confirmation, never silently filed, and not duplicating a recorded opportunity. Missing timing is gap-flagged, not invented.

cases.name: proposal-writer — draft grounded in cited past work, never filed
cases.criteria: Output is a proposal DRAFT (explicitly not filed to the brain) covering understanding/context, proposed approach, relevant experience, scope & deliverables, and next steps. Every claim that reuses past work cites a real source slug (deliverables/, engagements/, decisions/). When no analogous deliverable exists in the thin seed, that gap is flagged and the approach is drawn from the engagement record rather than a fabricated case study. Newly proposed scope and suggested team are flagged for human confirmation.

cases.name: deliverable-reuse — ranked, cited, gap-flags empty results
cases.criteria: Output is a ranked list of reusable deliverables/assets with title, client/engagement, date, what it is, why relevant, and a link to the source slug. Only type:deliverable/asset pages qualify (a note, meeting, or decision is never surfaced as a deliverable). When the thin seed holds no matching deliverable, the empty result is gap-flagged with what was searched rather than fabricated.

cases.name: expertise-mapper — wraps find_experts/whoknows, gap-flags thin coverage
cases.criteria: Output is a ranked candidate list sourced from the built-in find_experts op (gbrain whoknows) and does not re-rank or name anyone the op did not return. Each candidate carries a WHY citing a linking engagement/meeting/decision slug traced via backlinks. When the seed yields only low-scoring stakeholders or no candidates, the op is still run and the thin/empty result is gap-flagged, not padded with fabricated experts.
